sourceRole: External references support the gate technique and current official posture only; local blueprints and repository evidence remain authoritative.

sources.label: NHS App web integration
usedFor: SCAL and connection agreement evidence expectations
limited release, monthly data, annual assurance, and journey-change notice obligations
sources.borrowed: The exit gate requires operational readiness, sample-user limited release controls, monthly data pack posture, annual assurance readiness, and one-month or three-month journey-change lead-time handling.
sources.rejectedOrBounded: The gate does not claim live NHS App production approval; it gates the repository and typed non-production evidence now.

sources.label: NHS App integration standards
usedFor: clinical safety and data privacy certification posture
sources.borrowed: Accessibility, service standard, clinical safety, and privacy evidence are release-significant rather than cosmetic.
sources.rejectedOrBounded: External standards do not override local release-tuple and proof-battery requirements.

sources.label: NHS service manual accessibility
sources.borrowed: The board treats accessibility as an every-role release control and not a separate widget or afterthought.
sources.rejectedOrBounded: A manual audit is represented as launch-condition inheritance; repository proof still uses deterministic Playwright and contract evidence.

sources.label: NHS service manual accessibility testing
sources.borrowed: The gate uses executable browser proof and records remaining external review obligations as phase-launch conditions.
sources.rejectedOrBounded: Screenshots alone are not accepted as accessibility proof.

sources.label: Playwright trace viewer
sources.borrowed: 402 requires Playwright proof for the gate board and consumes traces from 399, 400, and 401.
sources.rejectedOrBounded: Trace files support review but do not replace machine-readable verdict files.

sources.label: Playwright browser contexts
sources.borrowed: The gate board proof uses an isolated browser context and reduced-motion settings.
sources.rejectedOrBounded: Browser isolation does not waive backend release-control checks.

sources.label: Playwright accessibility testing
sources.borrowed: Automated checks are useful but partial; the gate requires actual keyboard, semantic, and responsive evidence from 401.
sources.rejectedOrBounded: Automated accessibility evidence is not treated as full NHS App audit completion by itself.

sources.label: Playwright ARIA snapshots
sources.borrowed: ARIA snapshots from 401 are treated as semantic evidence for calm, degraded, and frozen route states.
sources.rejectedOrBounded: ARIA snapshots do not replace release guardrail and rollback proof.

sources.label: Playwright visual comparisons
sources.borrowed: Visual captures support release-board inspection when paired with deterministic selectors and text parity.
sources.rejectedOrBounded: Visual baselines are supporting evidence, not the formal verdict source.
